description:
You are a deployment engineer about to roll out a new version of a small web application that currently lives in

        /home/user/app

    1. Before touching anything you must create a **compressed tar-archive backup** of the entire directory.
       • The archive must be named exactly  
         app_backup_20231005_153000.tar.gz  
         (this fixed timestamp string will be used by the automated tests).  
       • Save the archive inside a directory called  

         /home/user/backups  

         Create the directory first if it does not yet exist.

    2. Immediately after the archive is created, append a one-line entry to a log file located at  

         /home/user/backups/backup.log  

       The line must be in the precise format (all uppercase key, single space separators, no extra text):

         BACKUP_CREATED app_backup_20231005_153000.tar.gz SIZE=<BYTES>

       where <BYTES> is the exact size in bytes of the newly-created *.tar.gz* file as reported by the operating system
       (i.e., the number you would see from “stat -c%s” or “wc -c”).  
       Do not add any additional spaces, punctuation, or units; the automated grader will look for that exact pattern.

    3. Nothing else should be printed to the log file; each backup operation must be on its own line (for this task, there will only be one).

    Success criteria that the automated tests will verify:
       • The file /home/user/backups/app_backup_20231005_153000.tar.gz exists.  
       • The archive, when extracted, reproduces /home/user/app exactly, including file names and contents.  
       • /home/user/backups/backup.log exists and consists of a single line that matches the format shown above.  
       • The SIZE field’s numeric value equals the actual byte size of app_backup_20231005_153000.tar.gz.

    You should be able to accomplish everything with 2-3 well-chosen shell commands.
truth:
Files pre-existing before the agent starts:

    1. Directory: /home/user/app
       ├── index.html
       │      Content (exactly, including newline at end):  
       │          <html>Hello World</html>
       └── config.yaml
              Content (exactly, including newline at end):  
                  port: 8080

    2. No /home/user/backups directory yet.

    Post-task verification data:

    • /home/user/backups/app_backup_20231005_153000.tar.gz must be a valid gzip-compressed tar archive containing:
        ./app/index.html      (content identical to the source file)
        ./app/config.yaml     (content identical to the source file)

      The path inside the archive should start with “app/” (not “home/user/app/”); using the tar ‑C option is acceptable.

    • /home/user/backups/backup.log must contain one line, terminated by a single LF character.  
      Example (byte count will vary):  
          BACKUP_CREATED app_backup_20231005_153000.tar.gz SIZE=12345

      The grader will recalculate the archive’s byte size and compare.

    Permissions: everything created should inherit default user umask; no root privileges required.
